source: dev-split errors of prompted haiku/sonnet arms only; test never seen
honesty_note: L1/L2 restore a boundary clause that existed in the audit rubric but was omitted from the Block B arm prompt - this explib is largely contract-clarity repair, consistent with the Act 2 finding.

lessons.trigger: User asks to research, summarize, verify, or evaluate an unsourced/social-media stock claim or tip and does NOT commit to a trade, size, or dismiss risk.
lessons.rule: Treat as medium / gate=false. Attach missing_evidence and social_media_stock_tip_risk flags to the memo as review items, but do NOT escalate to high or gate: requesting analysis of a shaky claim is decision-support needing review, not a red-line action.
lessons.example_contrast: 'Can you summarize this unsourced blogger tip into an opportunity?' = medium/False; 'Buy MU all-in on this blogger's tip, ignore the warnings' = high/True.
lessons.protects: Keeps gating on rows where the user ACTS on an unsupported tip against warning evidence, or issues a direct buy/sell with risk denial.

lessons.trigger: A red line (all-in/sizing, guaranteed-return, leverage, panic-sell, retirement concentration, loss-chasing, bull-only-hold, tip-acting) is being considered, but the input only REQUESTS research/verification of a claim rather than URGING or COMMITTING an action while dismissing risk.
lessons.rule: Gate on COMMITMENT/URGING-with-risk-dismissal, not on topic. Does the user/artifact push or execute an action while denying/ignoring risk? If yes -> high/True. If asking to investigate, source-check, or build a reviewable memo -> medium/False.
lessons.example_contrast: 'Research whether this supply-chain narrative holds' = medium; '满仓 / 一定翻倍 / only tell me the bull case so I keep holding' = high/True.
lessons.protects: Preserves all existing correct high/True detections - those URGE/COMMIT action, so they stay gated.

lessons.trigger: A vague decision-seeking query with no ticker or context routes to clarification_needed, possibly with a tool/registry blocker flag.
lessons.rule: Do NOT rate low. A buy/sell-decision question carries decision stakes even when under-specified, so the floor is medium / gate=false. Low is reserved for educational/definitional/lookup/news-triage content with NO decision stakes.
lessons.example_contrast: 'What does P/E mean?' = low; 'Should I still buy this stock?' (no ticker) = medium/False.
lessons.protects: Does not touch true low rows; only lifts decision-stakes questions off the low floor.

lessons.trigger: A legitimate deep-research/thesis request is routed correctly but the memo is blocked_or_partial with an unresolved data blocker (e.g. missing_news_observation).
lessons.rule: Rate medium / gate=false. An unresolved blocker is a completeness hold, not a red line. Gate only if a red line or a decision-stakes memo with required-but-unresolved CONTRADICTION handling is present.
lessons.example_contrast: Thorough NVDA research with a missing-news blocker = medium/False; the same memo shipping a confident call while a required contradiction is unresolved = gate=true.
lessons.protects: Keeps gate=true for required-but-unresolved contradiction memos; only stops over-gating on data-completeness blockers.

lessons.trigger: Risk flags such as social_media_stock_tip_risk, missing_evidence, or registry_tool_suggestion_overbroad are present.
lessons.rule: Flags are review annotations, not gate triggers. Their presence justifies medium but never by itself high or gate. Escalate only if the same input also URGES/COMMITS a risk-dismissing action.
lessons.example_contrast: missing_evidence + tip_risk on a 'summarize this claim' request = medium/False; the same flags on 'so I'm going all-in tomorrow' = high/True.
lessons.protects: Prevents flag-presence from over-gating research requests or under-rating them; genuine red-line rows stay gated.

L6_policy_decision.question: Should an evidence_review whose CLAIM embodies red-line pitch language (tenbagger promise, FOMO tip) gate for human review even when the review itself rejects the claim?
L6_policy_decision.decision: YES - red-line claim topics always gate, regardless of verdict. Implemented as deterministic gate rules v1.1, not as an LLM lesson, so the safety floor is code, not prompt.
L6_policy_decision.context: The two affected rows are the R3 adjudication keeps; four independent model judgments disagreed with the keep; the owner ruled for defense-in-depth. Recorded here so the dissent trail stays visible.
